stage_summary: Stage 8 adds repository-wide readers only after stewardship exists. The current repo has no `status` command, no `doctor` command, and no visible Domum reader surfaces. The rebased implementation plan limits this stage to disk-truth readers: status, doctor, maturity classification, health warnings, and continuity reporting grounded in visible files rather than orchestration theater. Because Stage 7 stewardship does not exist yet, every Stage 8 deliverable remains future work and depends on archive-aware reading surfaces first.
baseline_guardrails: Stage 1 Finis baseline is already implemented and remains the canonical goal layer.
Stage 2 Formam baseline is already implemented and remains the canonical deliverable and map layer.
Stage 3 Agenda baseline is already implemented and remains the canonical task, DAG, and task-log layer.
Post-baseline Stage 8 deliverables must report on visible repository truth after stewardship exists; they must not recreate the baseline engines or invent orchestration that bypasses on-disk state.

deliverables.title: Repository status command
deliverables.description: Add a repo-wide `status` reader that summarizes the current visible AZQ state from on-disk artifacts across the live engines.
deliverables.why_it_exists: The implementation plan explicitly names `status` as a Stage 8 reader, and the current CLI surface stops at `agenda` with no repository-wide reporting command.
acceptance_criteria: The CLI exposes a status reader that reports across Scintilla, Finis, Formam, Agenda, and any live stewardship artifacts.
The status output is derived from visible files and identifiers on disk rather than hidden process memory.
The status reader distinguishes implemented capability from the narrower checked-in artifact snapshot when relevant.
Tests verify at least one end-to-end repository status report against deterministic fixture state.

deliverables.title: Repository doctor command
deliverables.description: Add a repo-wide `doctor` reader that flags visible contradictions, missing links, and broken continuity across canonical AZQ artifacts.
deliverables.why_it_exists: The implementation plan names `doctor` as a Stage 8 reader, and there is currently no command that validates cross-engine storage truth in the live repo.
acceptance_criteria: The CLI exposes a doctor reader that can detect at least missing parent references, stale archive markers, or contradictory status surfaces from visible files.
Doctor findings cite exact artifact paths or identifiers so the operator can inspect the problem on disk.
Doctor reports remain read-only and do not silently repair repository state.
Tests cover at least one broken fixture state and one healthy fixture state.

deliverables.title: Repository maturity and continuity report
deliverables.description: Add a reader surface that classifies repository maturity and continuity from visible artifacts, including where code capability exceeds the checked-in data snapshot.
deliverables.why_it_exists: The rebased implementation plan and gap report both stress the distinction between code capability and checked-in artifact truth, and Stage 8 is where that should become a visible reader rather than hidden interpretation.
acceptance_criteria: A repo-wide reader can state which stages are represented by code capability and which are represented by checked-in artifacts.
The report cites concrete evidence such as artifact directories, canonical files, or missing artifact families.
Continuity reporting remains conservative when evidence is mixed or partial rather than overstating completion.
Tests or fixtures demonstrate output for a repo state where code capability and checked-in data differ.

deliverables.title: Visible repository health warnings
deliverables.description: Add a reader surface for warning about visible repo drift such as stale docs versus live code, missing canonical directories, or archive inconsistencies.
deliverables.why_it_exists: Stage 8 is defined as a disk-truth reporting stage, and the current repo already demonstrates real doc-versus-code tension that later readers should surface plainly.
acceptance_criteria: Reader output can flag at least one class of doc-versus-repo contradiction or canonical storage inconsistency from visible files.
Warnings are reviewable and cite the specific file or path evidence that triggered them.
Warnings remain separate from remediation tasks and do not silently mutate repository state.
Tests cover at least one warning-producing fixture and one clean fixture.
